name: Affix
goal: Wrap content in a position:sticky region pinned to a viewport edge with configurable offset and z-index.
description: Pure-CSS sticky wrapper for toolbar headers, bottom docks, and side-rail TOCs. Accepts any CSS length for the offset and uses logical inset properties (inset-inline-start/end) for RTL-safe start/end edges.
limitations: position:sticky requires the nearest scrolling ancestor to have overflow other than visible. The affix must be shorter than the scroll container to produce a visible sticking effect. The 'until' feature (unstick past a sentinel element) is deferred to v2 — it requires IntersectionObserver and was excluded from the pure-CSS v1 scope.

props.name: edge
props.description: Viewport edge to stick against. start/end use logical inset properties for RTL support.

props.name: offset
props.description: Distance from the sticky edge. A bare number is treated as pixels (64 → 64px); any CSS length string passes through unchanged.

props.name: z
props.description: z-index applied to the sticky element.

slots.description: Content to affix — typically a toolbar, filter bar, header, or CTA.

cssVariables.name: --_offset
cssVariables.description: Resolved offset value set by the component from the offset attribute. Consumed by :host([edge]) rules.

cssVariables.name: --_z
cssVariables.description: Resolved z-index value set by the component from the z attribute.

a11y.notes: No ARIA role is added; the element is transparent to the accessibility tree. Screen readers see the slotted content as if the wrapper were not present.
